advanced.title: Impeccable
advanced.meaning: Perfect; without any flaws
advanced.example: Her presentation was impeccable

advanced.title: Profound
advanced.meaning: Very deep meaning or impact
advanced.example: The book had a profound effect on the way I think

advanced.title: Inevitable
advanced.meaning: Unavoidable
advanced.example: With such dark clouds, rain seemed inevitable

advanced.title: Phenomenal
advanced.meaning: Extraordinary
advanced.example: She made a phenomenal recovery afer the incident

advanced.title: Articulate
advanced.meaning: Able to express ideas clearly
advanced.example: She gave an articulate explanation of the problem

advanced.title: Meticulous
advanced.meaning: Very careful about details
advanced.example: He kept meticulous records of every expense

advanced.title: Apprehensive
advanced.meaning: Slightly worried about what might happen
advanced.example: She felt apprehensive about speaking in front of the crowd

advanced.title: Perplexed
advanced.meaning: Confused and unsure
advanced.example: He looked perplexed by the sudden change in plans

advanced.title: Synergy
advanced.meaning: When people work together and produce a better result
advanced.example: The synergy between marketing and sales increased revenue

advanced.title: Cohesive
advanced.meaning: Well-connected and united
advanced.example: The team delivered a cohesive presentation

advanced.title: Strategic
advanced.meaning: Carefully planned to achieve a goal
advanced.example: He made a strategic investment

advanced.title: Viable
advanced.meaning: Practical and capable of working successfully
advanced.example: The business idea was financially viable

advanced.title: Skeptic
advanced.meaning: Someone who questions things instead of blindly believing
advanced.example: A good skeptic looks for evidence before agreeing

advanced.title: Mentor
advanced.meaning: A person who guides you towards growth
advanced.example: Behind every successful person is a mentor

advanced.title: Trailblazer
advanced.meaning: Someone who creates a path others follow
advanced.example: She became a trailblazer in a male-dominated industry

advanced.title: Underdog
advanced.meaning: The one n one expects to win...but does
advanced.example: Everyone loves an underdog story

advanced.title: Gaslighting
advanced.meaning: Manipulating someone into doubting reality
advanced.example: He kept denying what happened and made her feel like she was imagining things - That's gaslighting

advanced.title: Projection
advanced.meaning: Attributing your feelings to others
advanced.example: He accused her lying because he lies often - That's projection

advanced.title: Cognitive bias
advanced.meaning: A mental shortcut that can lead to errors in judgment
advanced.example: Believeing thefirst thing that you hear without questioning - It is a cognitive bias

advanced.title: Validaton
advanced.meaning: Acknowledging feelings
advanced.example: Instead of dismissing his worries, she listened and offered validation

advanced.title: Ephemeral
advanced.meaning: Lasting for a very short time
advanced.example: The beauty of a sunset is ephemeral

advanced.title: Serendipity
advanced.meaning: A pleasant discovery made by chance
advanced.example: Meeting her at the cafe was pure serendipity

advanced.title: Eloquent
advanced.meaning: Fluent and persuasive in speaking or writing
advanced.example: She gave an eloquent speech about climate change

advanced.title: Ubiquitous
advanced.meaning: Present anywhere
advanced.example: Smartphones have become ubiquitous in modern life
